summary:
The Pes Anserinus involves the tendon insertions of three particular thigh muscles onto the inner side of the lower leg bone; this distinct combination has no comparable counterpart at other anatomic sites such as joints, glands, or soft tissues mentioned. Therefore, while there may seem close analogies across different body parts due to general muscular anatomy principles, only "TMJ" remains within provided choices for its lack of identical configuration matching exactly what defines Pes Anserinus uniquely. Agent1 concludes based purely upon absence rather than presence similarity between structures presented against known pes ansersinu characteristics. 

Key Points Summarized:

- Pes Anserinus consists solely from Sartorius, Gracilis & Semitendinosus tendon convergences into one spot medially over proximal tibia.
- No parallel formation occurs identically near given alternatives including jaw joint etc., making choice “A” most fitting even if imperfectly aligned conceptually compared directly per strict definition criteria here applied clinically.
